channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Typhoon Krathon makes landfall in Taiwan, packing fierce winds and torrential rain
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Typhoon Krathon made landfall Thursday in Taiwan's major port city of Kaohsiung, bringing torrential rains and fierce winds to the island's south. Trees were brought down by high winds and roads flooded, prompting the closure of schools and businesses.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Taiwan hospital fire leaves at least 9 dead as typhoon batters island's south
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A fire at a hospital in southern Taiwan killed at least nine people Thursday as the island was being battered by a typhoon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgian parliament speaker signs anti-LGBTQ law after president refuses to sign it
item.description: The speaker of the Georgian parliament has signed into a law a bill that severely curtails LGBTQ+ rights in the country and mirrors legislation adopted in neighboring Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A small town in central Thailand is mourning the 23 who died in a school bus fire
item.description: A small town in central Thailand is preparing for a mass funeral for the 23 children and teachers who died in a bus fire while on a school trip.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In music and dance, Sudanese performers transport refugee audiences back home
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — As the performers took the stage and the traditional drum beat gained momentum, Sudanese refugees sitting in the audience were moved to tears. Hadia Moussa said the melody reminded her of the country's Nuba Mountains, her family's ancestral home.
item.dc:creator: BY FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bodies of the children and teachers who died in a Thai school bus fire return home for funeral
item.description: LAN SAK, Thailand (AP) — The bodies of almost two dozen young students and teachers who died in a bus fire in a suburb of Bangkok were returned late Wednesday to central Thailand, where they began their ill-fated school field trip.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dominican Republic will deport up to 10,000 Haitians a week, citing an 'excess' of immigrants
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Dominican Republic announced Wednesday that it would start massive deportations of Haitians living illegally in the country, expelling up to 10,000 of them a week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda records at least 9 deaths from Marburg outbreak
item.description: Rwanda has declared a public outbreak of the Marburg virus, confirming nearly 30 infections and at least nine deaths as of the end of September.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Chinese Communist Party marks 75 years of rule as economic, security threats persist
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule. The state-controlled media reported on China's progress and stability, with no mention of the many challenges facing the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: US destroyer fires ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel against Iranian barrage
item.description: The USS Cole and USS Bulkeley fired about a dozen ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel from a barrage of nearly 200 Iranian missiles on Tuesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel bans UN secretary-general over anti-Israel actions: 'Doesn't deserve to set foot on Israeli soil'
item.description: The United Nations has increasingly faced criticism over the past year for how officials have responded to and handled the conflicts in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden opposes Israeli attack on Iranian nuclear sites
item.description: President Biden said Wednesday that he does not support Israel carrying out strikes on Iranian nuclear sites as the U.S. and its allies mull additional sanctions against Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump remark about US troop injuries in Iraq 'obviously not accurate': Esper
item.description: Former Defense Secretary Mark Esper said Tuesday that former President Trump's recent remarks downplaying U.S. troop brain injuries in Iraq are “obviously not accurate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prince Harry visits the small mountain kingdom of Lesotho, where he's called 'the warrior'
item.description: MASERU, Lesotho (AP) — Prince Harry is visiting the small mountain kingdom of Lesotho having co-founded a youth charity there in 2006 in honor of his late mother, Princess Diana.
item.dc:creator: KEKETSO PHAKELA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ancient painting revealed in Egypt beneath layers of bird poop
item.description: During restoration of the Temple of Edfu, artwork was discovered beneath layers of bird poop, dust and soot covering the temple walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will Netanyahu 'take the fight directly to Iran' after massive missile strike across Israel?
item.description: Amid the most lethal Iranian missile strike into Israel territory, military experts are urging Israel to take off the gloves and punish the mullahs in Iran with devastating warfare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Iran’s missile attack on Israel felt like
item.description: HAIFA, Israel -- I knew right from the get-go this attack from Iran was going to be different than anything we had seen. Over the past year, I’ve personally witnessed countless rocket attacks on cities like Tel Aviv, Ashkelon and Sderot coming from Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, The Hill

item.title: An American bomb from WWII explodes at a Japanese airport, leaving a crater on the taxiway
item.description: A U.S. bomb from World War II has exploded at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway and the cancellation of more than 80 flights.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli military says regular infantry, armored units joining limited ground operation in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said regular infantry and armored units were joining what it has called its limited and localized ground operations in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum keeps doing morning briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum has begun her presidency the same way her predecessor concluded his: with a morning media briefing known in Mexico as a mañanera from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sánchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France's prime minister vows to curb debt as he faced baptism of fire at heckled parliament
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Lawmakers gave new French Prime Minister Michel Barnier a rough ride, heckling him constantly, as he laid out his proposed policies in an inaugural speech to parliament on Tuesday, a baptism of fire that highlighted how difficult governing will be for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Iran attacks ratchet up Middle East conflict: 5 takeaways
item.description: The Middle East is a powder keg after Iran launched nearly 200 missiles toward Israel Wednesday, with the United States vowing "severe consequences" for the action. No deaths in Israel were immediately assessed, and U.S. officials said the missiles — aimed at Israel and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: US readiness plays 'significant' role in fending off Iranian attack on Israel
item.description: The U.S. once again assured Israel it stands ready to defend it against Iran just hours prior to Tehran launching a second large scale strike on Israel in which some 200 missiles were fired at it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: IDF meets little resistance from Hezbollah after weeks of hitting terror targets, officials say
item.description: The Israel Defense Force met little resistance from Hezbollah terrorists despite weeks of growing anticipation of an Israeli incursion amid strikes and raids by specials ops units in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah terrorists engaged in sex slavery, rape, mass murder of Syrians
item.description: Startling new information has been released about Hezbollah’s enslavement and rapes of women amid Israel’s decapitation of senior Hezbollah terrorists over the last two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli ministers frustrated over US, IDF leak on Lebanon operation: Report
item.description: Israeli ministers agree to operational strategy for ground invasion hours after officials in the US confirmed a 'limited' invasion of Lebanon was coming, drawing frustration from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel launches limited ground operations in Lebanon as war against Hezbollah, terrorist groups continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) began limited operations in Lebanon on Monday evening, continuing its battle against Hezbollah terrorists. The military killed several key figures of the group over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Flooding and landslides in Nepal kill at least 66 people, with as many again still missing
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Flooding and landslides caused by continuous rainfall has killed at least 66 people in Nepal, and a further 69 are missing, officials said Saturday. Rain has been pouring down since Friday night and is expected to continue over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, WGN 9
